allow_instructions: Read-only inspection is fine: git status/diff/log/show, ls, cat, grep/rg, find, and reading files anywhere in the workspace.
Linters, type checkers, formatters, and test runners are fine (eslint, tsc, ruff, flake8, mypy, pytest, cargo clippy/test, go vet/test, golangci-lint).
Project builds and dependency installs scoped to the workspace are fine (npm/pnpm/yarn install and build, pip install into a venv, cargo build, go build).
Creating and writing files inside the workspace is fine.
block_instructions: Hold any destructive filesystem command for review: rm -rf, bulk deletes, or anything that could remove files outside the immediate task.
Hold git history-rewriting or remote-mutating commands: force pushes, hard resets of shared branches, branch/tag deletion, and any push to main/master.
Hold anything that touches credentials or machine config: SSH config, shell rc files, ~/.aws, ~/.config, environment secrets, or keychain.
Hold network calls that send data outward (curl/wget POST/PUT, uploads) or pipe a remote script into a shell.
Hold package publishing or deploys: npm publish, cargo publish, gh release, and production deploy commands.
